title: Spin-orbital exchange as a route to intertwined dipole-quadrupole orbital order in MnV$_2$O$_4$ under strong trigonal crystal field
abstract: Orbitally degenerate systems provide a promising platform for realizing novel quantum phases driven by spin-orbital exchange interactions, as described by the Kugel-Khomskii model. Spinel vanadates, in which orbital degrees of freedom remain active, exhibit structural and magnetic transitions accompanied by orbital ordering, but the nature of the orbital state in MnV$_2$O$_4$ remains under debate. Here, we combine first-principles calculations with an effective spin-orbital model to address this problem. We show that a significant trigonal crystal field is present in high-temperature cubic phase and plays an essential role in determining the low-energy degrees of freedom. Based on the resulting parameters, we construct an effective Hamiltonian beyond the conventional dominant-hopping approximation and demonstrate that subdominant hopping processes strongly modify the spin-orbital exchange interactions. As a result, the system stabilizes a two-in/two-out magnetic configuration featuring spin canting and intertwined dipole-quadrupole orbital order.
summary: This paper investigates how subdominant electron hopping and a strong trigonal crystal field drive complex spin-orbital ordering in MnV2O4. It demonstrates that these interactions stabilize a two-in/two-out magnetic structure with intertwined orbital dipole and quadrupole orders. This work provides a microscopic resolution to the long-standing debate regarding the orbital configuration in this spinel vanadate.
main_problem: Determining the microscopic origin of the intertwined dipole-quadrupole orbital order and the specific magnetic configuration in the spinel vanadate MnV2O4.
main_result: The inclusion of subdominant hopping processes and a strong trigonal crystal field stabilizes a two-in/two-out (2I2O) magnetic configuration with spin canting and ferroquadrupolar orbital order, resolving the debate over the orbital state.
method: The authors combine first-principles DFT calculations with a strong-coupling expansion and mean-field theory using SU(N) coherent states to derive and analyze an effective spin-orbital Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: The system is the spinel vanadate MnV2O4, specifically the V3+ ions on a pyrochlore lattice. The model is a multiorbital Kugel-Khomskii-type Hamiltonian including Coulomb interaction, trigonal crystal field, spin-orbit coupling, and electron hopping.
key_observables: Spin configurations (AIAO vs 2I2O), orbital dipole and quadrupole moments, orbital occupations, and spin canting angles.
important_parameters: Trigonal crystal field splitting (42 meV), spin-orbit coupling strength (27 meV), hopping integrals (t1-t4), and the tuning parameter x for subdominant hopping.
main_assumptions: Neglects intersite quantum fluctuations and single-site spin-orbital entanglement; assumes the validity of the effective low-energy manifold.
figures_summary: Figure 1a shows the spinel structure, pyrochlore lattice, and local trigonal axes.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the scientific problem and the physical system, followed by the construction of the multiorbital Hamiltonian using DFT-derived parameters, the derivation of the effective spin-orbital model via perturbation theory, a mean-field analysis of phase evolution, and a conclusion on the stabilized 2I2O state.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Crystal structure and low-energy degrees of freedom in MnV2O4. a Spinel structure showing the pyrochlore lattice formed by V ions and edge-sharing VO6 octahedra. The local trigonal axes are indicated by blue arrows. b Splitting of the t2g orbitals under the trigonal crystal field into an a1g singlet and an eπ g doublet, along with a schematic representation of the d2 electron configuration at the V site. The a1g orbital wave function is also illustrated, with positive and negative isosurfaces depicted in turquoise- blue and red, respectively. c Orbital wave functions in the eπ g manifold corresponding to the τ µ eigenstates (µ = X, Y, Z) with eigenvalue +1/2. Each τ µ eigenstate...

FIG. 2. Comparison between the model with only the dominant hopping and that with all hoppings. a Spin-orbital order obtained for only the dominant hopping (t3). The spin sector exhibits an all-in/all-out (AIAO) configuration with ⟨SZ⟩̸= 0 and ⟨SX⟩= ⟨SY ⟩= 0. The orbital sector shows finite dipole order ⟨τ Z⟩̸= 0 with vanishing quadrupole components ⟨τ X⟩= ⟨τ Y ⟩= 0, resulting in uniform orbital occupations ⟨nxy⟩= ⟨nyz⟩= ⟨nzx⟩, compatible with cubic symmetry. b Spin-orbital order obtained for all hoppings (t1, t2, t3, t4). The spin sector exhibits a two-in/two-out (2I2O) configuration with spin canting arising from finite transverse components ⟨SX⟩, ⟨SY ⟩̸= 0. In the orbital sector, in...

FIG. 3. Two-electron configurations under trigonal crystal field and SOC. Energy splitting of eπ g orbitals in- duced by SOC. In the resulting two-electron states, SOC selects LZ eff = ±1 states in the eπ g manifold, which are locked to SZ = ±1 through the λ∥τ ZSZ coupling. These states define the local “in” and “out” configurations along the trigonal axes.

FIG. 4. Phase diagram, orbital occupations and magnetic structure. a Mean-field phase diagram in the x–ζ plane, where x is the hopping tuning parameter and ζ is the SOC constant. b Orbital occupations ⟨nm⟩(m = yz, zx, xy) in each phase as a function of x at ζ = 27 meV. c Spin configuration in Phase III, showing the noncoplanar structure and canting toward the global xy plane. The canting angle shown in the figure is the value obtained at the point corresponding to MnV2O4. d Effective spin exchange constants J∥(blue) and J⊥(gray) as a function of x in Phase III under the 2I2O magnetic structure.

FIG. 5. Orbital occupations and effective quadrupolar field. a Orbital occupations ⟨nm⟩(m = yz, zx, xy) as a function of the angle φ that parametrizes the transverse orbital pseudospin τpl, defined by ( ⟨τ X⟩, ⟨τ Y ⟩) = |τpl|(cos φ, sin φ). b Effective orbital field κorb and its direction θ in Phase III as a function of the hopping tuning parameter x.
